# Microprocessor Systems
## BSc (Honours) in Computing (Games Design and Development)

![LIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/Limerick%20Institute%20of%20Technology-2015-red?style=flat-square&logo=data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
![Microprocessor Systems](https://img.shields.io/badge/Module-Microprocessor%20Systems-blue?style=flat-square)

<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/joeaoregan/Yr2-Microprocessor-Systems-Labs/master/Screenshots/lab1.jpg" title="Lab1 Intro to 68K"/>
<h6>Lab1 Intro to 68K</h6>

### Lab 1 – Intro to 68000 programming

In this lab you will learn how to write a simple 68000 program. Please include an introduction and a conclusion in your lab report along with code and analysis

1. - Launch easy68k (If it is not installed download and install it)
2. - Edit the default program to place the decimal value 10 in D1
3. - run the program by clicking project - assemble sources, execute
4. - click on the play icon and minimise the Sim68K DOS windows (its the one coloured black)
5. - Press f9 to run the program and record the value in D1. Why is it this value?
6. - Add to the program so it places the decimal value 9 in D4, execute and examine the value
7. - Add to the program so it adds D4 to D1 and stores the answer in D1
8. - Add to the program so it place the HEX value F in D2, execute etc
9. - Add to the program so it place the binary value 00010000 in D3, execute etc
10. - Increment the value in D1 by decimal value 3, execute and record values

Word count should be in the range of (300 – 1100 words) The number of diagrams/screenshots depends on the Lab requirements. The lab book will be an electronic document. If the word count is less than or greater than the range above it will not be accepted. Note source code is not included in the word count.

Each lab report should be saved in a separate word document. All diagrams should be labelled and a description provided e.g. figure 1 – screen shot of the simulator, code listing 1 – input section of the code

You will submit your lab reports according to the schedule in moodle

A lab report requires the following sections

**Introduction** – This should describe the purpose of the lab and the learning goal for the lab (What was the objective)

**Description**: This should describe what you did in the lab , what you had to learn: (What specific knowledge, information, skills,concepts,commands or methods did you need to acquire to complete the lab?)
It shoulf also explain how you determined if you had achieved that goal, (What criteria did you use to determine whether you have met your goal and completed the lab succesfully?)

**Conclusion**: This should summarise what you learned from doing this lab and describe the practical importance of this concept that you explored in this lab and how it may be put to use
